questions.question: What is the topic for Week 21?
options: C. HOW TO MANAGE YOUR TIME
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Twenty-One is TIME MANAGEMENT.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 20?
questions.explanation: Resourcefulness was the topic for Week 20.

questions.question: Redeeming the time, because the days are ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Ephesians 5:16) says 'because the days are evil.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Ephesians 5:16.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 21 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: Redeeming the time, because the days are evil.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Ephesians 5:16.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Ephesians 5:16), why should we be redeeming the time?
options: B. Because the days are evil
C. Because Jesus is coming soon
D. Because we only live once
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states 'because the days are evil.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, time is distributed differently based on age, race, and status.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Time is evenly distributed across board, irrespective of age, race, gender or status.'

questions.question: Which verse is cited in the introduction regarding time being of the essence to a man who counts it important to fulfill purpose?
questions.explanation: The introduction cites '(John 9:4)' for this point.

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'TIME MANAGEMENT DEFINED'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, time management is about making the best use of your time productively and efficiently because it is always ________.
questions.explanation: The text says '...because it is always limited (Ephesians 5:16).'

questions.question: Time management can be defined as the art of having time to do everything that you need to do without feeling what?
questions.explanation: The text states 'without feeling stressed about it (Exodus 18:18-23)...'

questions.question: Outline I suggests that you should just work hard and be busy, even if you have nothing to show for the hours used.
questions.explanation: The text says 'Do not just work hard but work smart. Do not just be busy without having something to show for the hours used...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding prioritising your tasks in order of importance?
questions.explanation: The text says 'and prioritising your tasks in order of importance (Matthew 6:33).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'HOW TO MANAGE YOUR TIME'.

questions.question: Which scripture supports the statement that 'Every purpose in life has a time attached'?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Ecclesiastes 3:1)' right after that statement.

questions.question: According to the lesson, setting 'smart goals' means they should be all of the following EXCEPT?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'specific, measurable, attainable, relevant and time-bound'. 'Spiritual' is not listed there.

questions.question: According to the lesson, saying 'No' sometimes is an act of disrespect to others.
questions.explanation: The text states 'Saying ‘No’ sometimes is an act of respect for your person.'

questions.question: How does the lesson define 'Discipline' in the context of time management?
options: B. The effective use of your time, energy and resources in the pursuit of your task
C. Punishing yourself when you make a mistake
D. Waking up very early every morning
questions.explanation: Point 3 says 'Discipline is the effective use of your time, energy and resources in the pursuit of your task (Job 36:10).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding knowing when to say 'NO'?
questions.explanation: Point 4 cites '(James 5:12)' regarding knowing when to say NO.

questions.question: Which verse is associated with staying focused and shunning distractions?
questions.explanation: Point 2 says 'Stay focused: Shun Distractions (Philippians 3:13).'

questions.question: What does the conclusion of the lesson say a lived life is?
questions.explanation: The conclusion states: 'A life lived is an accumulation of time.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'See then that you walk circumspectly, not as fools but as wise,'
questions.verse_text: See then that you walk circumspectly, not as fools but as wise,
questions.explanation: This is verse 15 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Ephesians 5:15), how are we instructed to walk?
options: C. Circumspectly, not as fools but as wise
D. Quickly, because time is short
questions.explanation: Verse 15 reads 'See then that you walk circumspectly, not as fools but as wise'.

questions.question: According to Ephesians 5:17, what should we understand instead of being unwise?
options: A. The mysteries of the universe
B. What the will of the Lord is
C. The signs of the times
questions.explanation: Verse 17 says 'Therefore do not be unwise, but understand what the will of the Lord is.'

questions.question: According to the lesson, if you always say 'Yes' to everyone or everything, it will take you to the grave early in life.
questions.explanation: Point 4 explicitly states 'You cannot always say ‘Yes’ to everyone or everything. It will take you to the grave early in life...'

questions.question: How does the lesson define being 'punctual'?
options: A. Leaving early before an event finishes
B. Being available to do something at the agreed or proper time
C. Working faster than everyone else
questions.explanation: Point 3 says 'Being punctual is being available to do something at the agreed or proper time (Esther 9:27).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited in connection with making the best use of time productively and efficiently?
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'making the best use of your time productively and efficiently (Proverbs 6:6-11)...'

questions.question: Until you understand the ________ of time, you cannot succeed maximally in life.
questions.explanation: The introduction states 'Until you understand the value of time, you cannot succeed maximally in life.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding having time to do everything without feeling stressed about it?
questions.explanation: The text says 'without feeling stressed about it (Exodus 18:18-23)...'

questions.question: When time is wasted, the ________ is no longer guaranteed.
questions.explanation: The text says 'Hence, when time is wasted, the purpose is no longer guaranteed.'
